text:
Boxing fans will have been tuning in to catch the action at theParis2024Olympicsover the past week.

And you don’t need to be eagle-eyed to have noticed that male boxers haven’t been wearing headguards during their rounds, while the women boxers have been donning red and blue headgear.

You’d also be forgiven for being curious about the difference, and if there is a particular health and safety reason for it.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Here’s what we know.

Female boxers wear headguards at the Olympics because of rules set by the Olympic Committee (IOC) and the International Boxing Association (IBA).

Before the 2016 Olympics in Rio, both male and female athletes were made to wear headguards to improve their safety and to reduce concussions.

The decision was made after South Korean Fighter Kim Duk-Koo died after a world championship bout in 1982.

The IOC and IOA subsequently mandated that fighters at the Olympics, who are amateurs, must wear head protection for their own safety.

However, this changed in 2016 when a study from the Global Sport Institute revealed that boxers fighting with no head gear were less likely to suffer concussions.

This allowed men to fight without headgear, but given the study was only conducted on males, the IOC decided that women should continue to wear headgear out of an abundance of caution.

In 2013, theWall Street Journalwrote that then-chairman of the AIBA medical commission, Dr Charles Butler, explained that concussion was less of an issue with women fighters due to their strength.

However, in 2021,Naturereported that women sports players are at greater risk of traumatic brain injuries, and often ‘fare worse’ after a concussion, than their male counterparts.

Following the rule change, an observationalstudy published in 2017concurred that ‘the number of stoppages due to head blows was significantly decreased without headguards’ in male boxing.

All minors (boys and girls), schoolchildren, juniors, youth and Senior Elite and Senior Development Females must also fight with headwear.

title: Why do women wear headguards but not men in Olympic boxing? | Metro News
description: Find out why female boxers wear headgear during Olympic boxing matches at Paris 2024, and why male boxers don't wear head protection.
